title: jacea nervosa
intro: Jacea nervosa, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include acting as an antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant. The plant's health benefits include helping to relieve menstrual cramps, reduce anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and coughs. Jacea nervosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dried powder, making it a versatile addition to various herbal remedies.
